DISCOVERY 302, PLAINTIFF LEROY GATLIN'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER AND TO QUASH DEPOSITION SUBPOENAS AND FOR AN AWARD OF SANCTIONS. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - GRANTED IN LARGE PART. THE SUBPOENAS ARE LIMITED TO MEDICAL RECORDS FROM JANUARY 1, 2006, TO THE PRESENT THAT CONCERN COUGHING, HEADACHES, SHORTNESS OF BREATH, DISORIENTATION, AND EXPOSURE TO CARBON MONOXIDE AND OTHER CHEMICALS OR TOXICS. LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT CORNELY COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING - DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 2ND CAUSE OF ACTION IS OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS MADE REFERENCE TO SPECIFIC ACTS AS TO SPECIFIC DEFENDANTS THAT ARE NOT INCONSISTENT OR VAGUE. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 10TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS TO SUPPORT THE REQUIRED ELEMENTS FOR THIS CAUSE OF ACTION. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 11TH CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO THIS DEFENDANT. (MOVING PARTY TO PREPARE A PROPOSED FORM OF ORDER.) JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS CHRISTINE BELL, TRUSTEE OF THE BELL LIVING TRUST, DAVID BELL, MARY BELL, TRUSTEE OF THE 1988 BELL DECLARATION OF TRUST, AND SHARON BELL'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS MODIFIED - DEFENDANTS' DEMURRER TO THE 1ST CAUSE OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST SEPARATE OUT THE NEGLIGENT HIRING CLAIM. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 3RD-6TH CAUSES OF ACTION IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD SPECIFICS OF THE CONTRACT OR ATTACH A COPY TO THE AMENDED COMPLAINT. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 8TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR NUISANCE IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND AS IT IS DUPLICATIVE OF THE NEGLIGENCE CLAIM. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 9TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR VIOLATION OF THE RENT ORDINANCE IS SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS TO SHOW THAT DEFENDANT ACTED WILLFULLY IN BAD FAITH. DEFENDANT'S DEMURRER TO THE 10TH CAUSE OF ACTION FOR I.I.E.D. IS SUSTAINTED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. PLAINTIFF MUST PLEAD FACTS SUFFICIENT TO SUPPORT THIS CAUSE OF ACTION. ORDER ON DEMURRER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. JUDGE: LORETTA M. GIORGI, REPORTER: KARLA ELLIS-DAVIS, CSR #12998
MINI-MINUTES FOR JUL-13-2011 9:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Protective Roder And To Quash Deposition Subpoenas And For An Award Of Sanctions; Separate Statement
Off Calendar Nov-03-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF LEROY GATLIN'S MOTION FOR PROTECTIVE ORDER AND TO QUASH DEPOSITION SUBPOENAS AND FOR AN AWARD OF SANCTIONS.

APPEARANCES: Ryan Vlasak, Esq. of Bracamontes & Vlasak 835-6777 for pltf & moving party; no other appearances.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Granted in large part. The subpoenas are limited to medical records from January 1, 2006, to the present that concern coughing, headaches, shortness of breath, disorientation, and exposure to carbon monoxide and other chemicals or toxics. Sanctions denied. Order signed in open court. Judge: Harold Kahn; Not reported

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-29-2011 Continued To Sep-30-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 610

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-13-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT CORNELY COMPANY'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Michael Bracamontes, Esq. of Bracamontes & Vlasak 835-6777 for pltf & opposing party; Francisco A. Gutierrez, Esq. of M C Hall & Associates 512-9865 for Bell defts.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant's demuurrer to the 2nd cause of action is overruled. Plaintiff has made reference to specific acts as to specific defendants that are not inconsistent or vague. Defendant's demurrer to the 10th cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts to support the required elements for this cause of action. Defendant's demurrer to the 11th cause of action is sustained without leave to amend as to this defendant. (Moving party to prepare a proposed form of order.) (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-13-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANT CORNELY COMPANY'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Michael Bracamontes, Esq. of Bracamontes & Vlasak 835-6777 for pltf & opposing party; Francisco A. Gutierrez, Esq. of M C Hall & Associates 512-9865 for Bell defts.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendant's motion to strike the punitive damages claim is granted with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts to support this claim. Defendant's motion to strike the prayer for attorney fees, restitution of rent and the treble damages is granted as to this defendant. Defendant's motion to strike the prejudgment interest claim is granted without leave to amend. (PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE ORDER.) (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-13-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS CHRISTINE BELL, Trustee of The Bell Living Trust, DAVID BELL, MARY BELL, Trustee of The 1988 Bell Declaration of Trust, and SHARON BELL'S DEMURRER TO FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Michael Bracamontes, Esq. of Bracamontes & Vlasak 835-6777 for pltf & opposing party; Francisco A. Gutierrez, Esq. of M C Hall & Associates 512-9865 for defts & moving parties.

RULING: Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified - Defendants' demurrer to the 1st cause of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must separate out the negligent hiring claim. Defendant's demurrer to the 3rd-6th causes of action is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead specifics of the contract or attach a copy to the amended complaint. Defendant's demurrer to the 8th cause of action for nuisance is sustained with leave to amend as it is duplicative of the negligence claim. Defendant's demurrer to the 9th cause of action for violation of the rent ordinance is sustained with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts to show that defendant acted willfully in bad faith. Defendant's demurrer to the 10th cause of action for I.I.E.D. is sustainted with leave to amend. Plaintiff must plead facts sufficient to support this cause of action. Order on demurrer signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998

MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jul-13-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
DEFENDANTS CHRISTINE BELL, Trustee of The Bell Living Trust, DAVID BELL, MARY BELL, Trustee of The 1988 Bell Declaration of Trust, and SHARON BELL'S MOTION TO STRIKE PORTIONS OF THE FIRST AMENDED COMPLAINT.

APPEARANCES: Michael Bracamontes, Esq. of Bracamontes & Vlasak 835-6777 for pltf & opposing party; Francisco A. Gutierrez, Esq. of M C Hall & Associates 512-9865 for defts & moving parties.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Defendants' motion to strike the punitive damages claims is granted with leave to amend. Defendants' motion to strike the allegations regarding violation of the rent ordinance is granted with leave to amend. Plaintiff must allege facts to show defendant acted willfully in bad faith. Order on motion to strike signed in open court. (302/LMG) Judge: Loretta M. Giorgi, Reporter: Karla Ellis-Davis, CSR #12998
